name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
        - Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity (nu_h=1e-19, p=4, 8th-order); RK4 on nonlinear term only
        - Forcing: stochastic white-in-time at k∈[3,5], calibrate force_amp empirically to achieve epsilon_inj=0.1
        - Wavenumber convention: integer wavenumbers via torch.fft.fftfreq(N, d=1.0/N); de-alias with 2/3 rule
        - Spinup: run until T/T_L >= 10, saving diagnostics only; monitor k_peak and T_L
        - Production: run for T_prod = 50 * T_L_spinup_end; seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly at start of production
        - Tracer advection: GPU grid_sample bilinear interpolation at every tracer snapshot step (dt_snap=0.05); record positions and velocities
        - Save Eulerian velocity/vorticity snapshots coarsened 1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4) at dt_vel=2.0
        - Save energy spectrum E(k,t) at every tracer snapshot; save diagnostics structured array
        - NaN/Inf guard after each step; verify U_rms*dt_snap < 0.1 before production
        - Write all output files to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/ with exact shapes specified
        - Save sim_params.json with all key parameters
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Data quality verification and inverse cascade validation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; verify < 0.3 * 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag if exceeded
        - Compute VACF C_vv(τ) at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a single figure with 4 panels or overlaid curves; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
        - Print all key statistics (mean displacement, VACF values, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction) to console
        - Save verification plot to data directory

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis of Lévy exponents across overlapping time windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer positions, velocities, times, and diagnostics
        - Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step ΔW=2*T_L; compute ~26 windows
        - For each window: compute displacement PDFs at lags τ={0.5,1,2}*T_L; fit α via McCulloch quantile method (ν_α = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25), interpolate from McCulloch 1986 table); also compute Hill estimator as cross-check
        - For each window: fit MSD(τ) power law to get γ(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get ν(t)
        - Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics for each window center
        - Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series
        - Fit α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares
        - Save all window statistics (alpha_mcculloch, alpha_hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers) to a numpy/npz file
        - Print all fitted parameters, stationarity test p-values, and key statistics to console

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Plot non-stationarity analysis results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load saved window statistics from Step 3
        - Generate multi-panel figure: α(t) with McCulloch and Hill estimates, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t), d_vv(t) all vs time
        - Generate scatter plot of α vs k_peak/k_box with fitted curve α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid
        - Annotate plots with stationarity test results (ADF/KPSS p-values)
        - Save plots to data directory

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256) and vel_times.npy
        - Apply 2D DWT using db4 wavelet (periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot at levels j=1,...,6 using PyWavelets
        - For each level j: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|²; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j); integrate until C_E < 0.1 to get T_eddy(j); apply Okubo-Weiss masking (Q < -Q_75th percentile) when computing spatial average
        - Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min
        - Re-advect 5000 tracers (seeded at same initial positions as DNS production run) through each filtered field using GPU-accelerated RK4 with linear temporal interpolation between snapshots
        - For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law, ν_VACF(j) from VACF decay
        - Save T_eddy(j), α(j), γ_MSD(j), ν_VACF(j) for all j to npz file
        - Print all T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j) to console
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load wavelet results from Step 5 and window statistics from Step 3
        - Plot α(j) vs T_eddy(j) with fitted curve; annotate with CTRW prediction α = 1+β/(2-β) where β ∝ T_eddy^δ; report δ
        - Plot γ_MSD(j) and ν_VACF(j) vs T_eddy(j)
        - Synthesis: compare α(t) from Task 2 with α(j_peak(t)) from wavelet analysis at dominant scale j corresponding to k_peak(t)
        - Generate 2D heatmap of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box) using all available data points
        - Save all synthesis plots to data directory

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Summarize DNS simulation parameters and spinup/production run outcomes (T_L, k_peak evolution, U_rms)
        - Report data quality verification: mean displacement per step, VACF at short lags, τ_corr
        - Describe inverse cascade development: k_peak drift from ~4 toward ~1-2 over T_prod
        - Report non-stationarity analysis: α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; ADF/KPSS test outcomes; fitted α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c parameters
        - Report wavelet decomposition results: T_eddy(j) for each level, α(j), γ(j), ν(j); fitted scaling α vs T_eddy; CTRW prediction test with δ exponent
        - Synthesis: comparison of α(t) vs α(j_peak(t)); 2D map interpretation; conclusion on stationarity
        - Discuss consistency with prior v1 results (α ≈ 1.4) and physical predictions

- Step 5:

name: plan_reviewer
content:
**Recommendations:**

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The forcing band in the research plan instructions specifies k∈[3,5] (matching the problem description fix for Iteration 0), but the engineering notes in the plan header specify k_force_min=10, k_force_max=12. The Step 1 instructions must explicitly resolve this contradiction by mandating k∈[3,5] as stated in the Simulation Design section, since the goal is to observe the inverse cascade developing from k~4 toward k~1. The instructions should also explicitly state that nu_h=1e-19 (not 3.9e-31) and that dt_snap=0.05 (not 0.01), consistent with the Simulation Design section rather than the conflicting engineering notes.

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The instructions should explicitly require that the initial tracer positions (at the start of production) be saved separately as a dedicated file (e.g., tracer_initial_positions.npy), since Step 5 requires re-seeding tracers at the exact same positions for wavelet-filtered re-advection. Without this, Step 5 cannot reproduce the identical initial conditions.

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The instructions should specify that the energy spectrum saved in energy_spectrum.npy must use integer wavenumber bins (k=1,2,...,N//2) with azimuthal averaging, and that k_peak is extracted as the wavenumber of maximum E(k) excluding k=0, so that the inverse cascade drift is unambiguously trackable in downstream steps.

- Step 2 (Data quality): The instructions should specify that the VACF normalization is C_vv(τ) = ⟨v(t)·v(t+τ)⟩ / ⟨v(t)·v(t)⟩, computed by averaging over all tracers and all reference times t within the production run, and that the decorrelation time τ_corr is defined as the first lag at which C_vv drops below 1/e, to ensure consistent reporting across steps.

- Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): The instructions should explicitly define the McCulloch table lookup implementation: since the full McCulloch (1986) table is not a standard library function, the engineer must implement a hardcoded lookup table of ν_α values at the standard quantile ratios, with bilinear interpolation in the (ν_α, ν_β) plane. The instructions should also specify that α must be clipped to [1.0, 2.0] after interpolation to avoid unphysical values, and that windows with fewer than 500 displacement samples per lag should be flagged and excluded from the stationarity tests.

- Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): The instructions should clarify that displacements for the McCulloch and Hill estimators are computed as the net displacement vector magnitude |r(t+τ) - r(t)| (not the incremental step), and that x and y components are treated separately (each providing N_tracers samples), then pooled, to maximize sample size per window.

- Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): The instructions should specify that the Okubo-Weiss parameter Q is computed from the coarsened 256×256 velocity snapshots (not the full 1024² field), using finite differences for the strain-rate and vorticity components, and that the masking threshold Q_threshold is computed once from the time-averaged |Q| field (not per-snapshot) to ensure temporal consistency of the mask across the T_eddy integration.

- Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): The instructions should add an explicit memory management requirement: since velocity_snapshots.npy may be several GB, the wavelet decomposition loop over snapshots should process one snapshot at a time (not load all into RAM simultaneously), and the reconstructed filtered fields for re-advection should be stored as memory-mapped arrays or processed in chunks to avoid OOM errors on the 128 GB RAM system.

- Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): The re-advection instructions should specify that the RK4 integration uses the vel_times.npy array for temporal interpolation weights (not assuming uniform spacing), and that tracers reaching the domain boundary are wrapped periodically using modulo 2π before each interpolation call to grid_sample.

- Step 6 (Wavelet scaling plots): The instructions should specify that the CTRW fit procedure first extracts β(j) from α(j) via the inverse relation β = 2(α-1)/(α) (derived from α = 1+β/(2-β)), then fits log(β) vs log(T_eddy) to obtain δ via linear regression, and that the fitted curve overlaid on the α(j) vs T_eddy(j) plot should show both the direct fit to α and the CTRW-predicted curve from the β power law, so the two can be visually compared.

- Step 7 (Results section): The researcher instructions should explicitly require reporting the numerical values of all fitted parameters with uncertainties (confidence intervals from the nonlinear least squares fits), the ADF and KPSS test statistics and p-values for each time series, the R² or goodness-of-fit metrics for the α vs T_eddy scaling, and a quantitative comparison of the measured α range against the prior v1 result of α ≈ 1.4 ± 0.1, including whether the v2 result falls within or outside that uncertainty band.

name: reviewer_response_evaluator
content:
**Recommendations:**

Step 1 (DNS simulation): The instructions must resolve the contradiction between the research plan and engineering notes by mandating k in [3,5], nu_h=1e-19, and dt_snap=0.05 as per the Simulation Design section.

Step 1 (DNS simulation): Require that initial tracer positions be saved to a dedicated file (e.g., tracer_initial_positions.npy) to ensure reproducibility for Step 5.

Step 1 (DNS simulation): Specify that the energy spectrum must use integer wavenumber bins with azimuthal averaging and define k_peak as the wavenumber of maximum E(k) excluding k=0.

Step 2 (Data quality): Define the VACF normalization as C_vv(tau) = <v(t)·v(t+tau)> / <v(t)·v(t)> and specify that the decorrelation time tau_corr is the first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e.

Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): Require a hardcoded McCulloch lookup table with bilinear interpolation, clip alpha to [1.0, 2.0], and exclude windows with fewer than 500 samples.

Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): Clarify that displacements are computed as net displacement vector magnitudes and that x and y components are pooled to maximize sample size.

Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): Specify that the Okubo-Weiss parameter Q is computed from 256x256 snapshots and that the masking threshold is derived from the time-averaged |Q| field.

Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): Implement memory management by processing snapshots one at a time and using memory-mapped arrays or chunks to prevent OOM errors.

Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): Require RK4 integration to use vel_times.npy for temporal interpolation and enforce periodic boundary wrapping using modulo 2pi.

Step 6 (Wavelet scaling plots): Specify the CTRW fit procedure by extracting beta from alpha, fitting log(beta) vs log(T_eddy), and plotting both the direct fit and the CTRW-predicted curve.

Step 7 (Results section): Require reporting of numerical values with uncertainties, ADF/KPSS test statistics, goodness-of-fit metrics, and a quantitative comparison against the prior v1 result of alpha approx 1.4 +/- 0.1.

name: review_recorder
content:
Recommendations have been logged.
Number of feedback rounds left: 0.
Now, update the plan accordingly, planner!

name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
        - Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity with nu_h=1e-19, p=4 (8th-order); RK4 on nonlinear term only
        - Forcing: stochastic white-in-time at k∈[3,5] (NOT k∈[10,12] — use Simulation Design values), calibrate force_amp empirically to achieve epsilon_inj=0.1
        - Wavenumber convention: integer wavenumbers via torch.fft.fftfreq(N, d=1.0/N); de-alias with 2/3 rule
        - Spinup: run until T/T_L >= 10, saving diagnostics only; monitor k_peak and T_L
        - Production: run for T_prod = 50 * T_L_spinup_end; seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly at start of production; save initial tracer positions to tracer_initial_positions.npy for reproducibility in Step 5
        - Tracer advection: GPU grid_sample bilinear interpolation at every tracer snapshot step with dt_snap=0.05 (NOT 0.01); record positions and velocities; enforce periodic boundary conditions via modulo 2π
        - Save Eulerian velocity/vorticity snapshots coarsened 1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4) at dt_vel=2.0
        - Save energy spectrum E(k,t) at every tracer snapshot using integer wavenumber bins (k=1,2,...,N//2) with azimuthal averaging; k_peak defined as argmax E(k) excluding k=0
        - Save diagnostics structured array with fields (time, E_total, E_rms, omega_rms, T_L, k_peak, d_vv_estimate)
        - NaN/Inf guard after each step; verify U_rms*dt_snap < 0.1 before production
        - Write all output files to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/ with exact shapes specified
        - Save sim_params.json with all key parameters including N, nu_h, epsilon_inj, dt_snap, dt_vel, T_spinup, T_prod, T_L_at_start_of_production
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Data quality verification and inverse cascade validation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step using periodic-aware distance (wrap differences to [-π, π]); verify < 0.3 * 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag if exceeded
        - Compute VACF C_vv(τ) = ⟨v(t)·v(t+τ)⟩ / ⟨v(t)·v(t)⟩ averaged over all tracers and all reference times; evaluate at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr as first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a single figure with overlaid curves; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
        - Print all key statistics (mean displacement, VACF values at each lag, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction) to console
        - Save verification plot to data directory

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis of Lévy exponents across overlapping time windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer positions, velocities, times, and diagnostics
        - Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step ΔW=2*T_L; compute ~26 windows
        - For each window: compute net displacement magnitudes |r(t+τ) - r(t)| at lags τ={0.5,1,2}*T_L; treat x and y components separately then pool to maximize sample size; exclude windows with fewer than 500 displacement samples per lag
        - Fit α via McCulloch quantile method: compute ν_α = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25); implement hardcoded McCulloch (1986) lookup table with bilinear interpolation in the (ν_α, ν_β) plane; clip α to [1.0, 2.0] after interpolation
        - Also compute Hill estimator α_Hill as cross-check using top-k order statistics
        - For each window: fit MSD(τ) power law to get γ(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get ν(t)
        - Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics for each window center
        - Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; print test statistics and p-values
        - Fit α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares; print fitted parameters with uncertainties
        - Save all window statistics (alpha_mcculloch, alpha_hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers, adf_results, kpss_results) to npz file
        - Print all fitted parameters, stationarity test p-values, and key statistics to console

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Plot non-stationarity analysis results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load saved window statistics from Step 3
        - Generate multi-panel figure: α(t) with both McCulloch and Hill estimates shown, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t), d_vv(t) all vs time window center
        - Generate scatter plot of α vs k_peak/k_box with fitted curve α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid; include confidence band
        - Annotate plots with ADF/KPSS test p-values and stationarity conclusions
        - Save plots to data directory

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy as memory-mapped array (not fully into RAM); load vel_times.npy
        - Apply 2D DWT using db4 wavelet (periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot one at a time to avoid OOM; extract detail coefficients at levels j=1,...,6 using PyWavelets
        - Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q from 256×256 velocity snapshots using finite differences; compute masking threshold once from time-averaged |Q| field (75th percentile); apply mask Q < -Q_threshold when computing spatial averages for T_eddy
        - For each level j: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|²; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over masked pixels; integrate until C_E < 0.1 (max lag = min(T_prod/4, 50*dt_vel)) to get T_eddy(j)
        - Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; store as memory-mapped arrays or process in chunks
        - Re-advect 5000 tracers seeded at positions from tracer_initial_positions.npy through each filtered field using GPU-accelerated RK4; use vel_times.npy for temporal interpolation weights (not assuming uniform spacing); enforce periodic boundary wrapping via modulo 2π before each grid_sample call
        - For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law, ν_VACF(j) from VACF decay
        - Save T_eddy(j), α(j), γ_MSD(j), ν_VACF(j) for all j to npz file
        - Print all T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j) to console
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load wavelet results from Step 5 and window statistics from Step 3
        - Extract β(j) from α(j) via inverse relation β = 2*(α(j)-1)/α(j) (derived from α = 1+β/(2-β)); fit log(β) vs log(T_eddy) via linear regression to obtain scaling exponent δ; print δ with uncertainty
        - Plot α(j) vs T_eddy(j): show data points, direct power-law fit to α, and CTRW-predicted curve from β power law overlaid for visual comparison; annotate with δ
        - Plot γ_MSD(j) and ν_VACF(j) vs T_eddy(j) on separate panels
        - Synthesis panel: compare α(t) from Step 3 with α(j_peak(t)) from wavelet analysis at dominant scale j corresponding to k_peak(t) at each window center
        - Generate 2D heatmap of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box) using all available data points from both analyses
        - Save all synthesis plots to data directory

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Summarize DNS simulation parameters (N=1024, k_force∈[3,5], nu_h=1e-19, epsilon_inj=0.1, dt_snap=0.05) and spinup/production run outcomes (T_L value, k_peak evolution, U_rms)
        - Report data quality verification with numerical values: mean displacement per step vs threshold 1.88, VACF values at lags 1,2,4,8,16 steps, τ_corr value, and whether C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3 criterion was met
        - Describe inverse cascade development quantitatively: k_peak values at t=0.1,0.3,0.6,1.0×T_prod
        - Report non-stationarity analysis with full numerical results: α(t) range and mean, γ(t) range, ν(t) range; ADF test statistics and p-values for each series; KPSS test statistics and p-values; fitted parameters a, b, c with confidence intervals for α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c; R² of fit
        - Report wavelet decomposition results: T_eddy(j) for j=1,...,6; α(j), γ(j), ν(j) for each filtered field; fitted scaling exponent δ with uncertainty; R² of β vs T_eddy fit; whether CTRW prediction α = 1+β/(2-β) is supported
        - Synthesis: quantitative comparison of α(t) vs α(j_peak(t)); interpretation of 2D α map; conclusion on stationarity of Lévy exponent
        - Quantitative comparison against prior v1 result α ≈ 1.4 ± 0.1: report whether v2 α values fall within or outside this band, and discuss physical interpretation of any differences

name: planner_response_evaluator
content:
**PLAN**

- Step 1:
	* sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
		- Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity with nu_h=1e-19, p=4 (8th-order); RK4 on nonlinear term only
		- Forcing: stochastic white-in-time at k∈[3,5] (NOT k∈[10,12] — use Simulation Design values), calibrate force_amp empirically to achieve epsilon_inj=0.1
		- Wavenumber convention: integer wavenumbers via torch.fft.fftfreq(N, d=1.0/N); de-alias with 2/3 rule
		- Spinup: run until T/T_L >= 10, saving diagnostics only; monitor k_peak and T_L
		- Production: run for T_prod = 50 * T_L_spinup_end; seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly at start of production; save initial tracer positions to tracer_initial_positions.npy for reproducibility in Step 5
		- Tracer advection: GPU grid_sample bilinear interpolation at every tracer snapshot step with dt_snap=0.05 (NOT 0.01); record positions and velocities; enforce periodic boundary conditions via modulo 2π
		- Save Eulerian velocity/vorticity snapshots coarsened 1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4) at dt_vel=2.0
		- Save energy spectrum E(k,t) at every tracer snapshot using integer wavenumber bins (k=1,2,...,N//2) with azimuthal averaging; k_peak defined as argmax E(k) excluding k=0
		- Save diagnostics structured array with fields (time, E_total, E_rms, omega_rms, T_L, k_peak, d_vv_estimate)
		- NaN/Inf guard after each step; verify U_rms*dt_snap < 0.1 before production
		- Write all output files to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/ with exact shapes specified
		- Save sim_params.json with all key parameters including N, nu_h, epsilon_inj, dt_snap, dt_vel, T_spinup, T_prod, T_L_at_start_of_production

- Step 2:
	* sub-task: Data quality verification and inverse cascade validation
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy
		- Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step using periodic-aware distance (wrap differences to [-π, π]); verify < 0.3 * 2π ≈ 1.88; print result and flag if exceeded
		- Compute VACF C_vv(τ) = ⟨v(t)·v(t+τ)⟩ / ⟨v(t)·v(t)⟩ averaged over all tracers and all reference times; evaluate at lags τ = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 steps; verify C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3; report decorrelation time τ_corr as first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e
		- Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a single figure with overlaid curves; confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
		- Print all key statistics (mean displacement, VACF values at each lag, τ_corr, k_peak at each time fraction) to console
		- Save verification plot to data directory

- Step 3:
	* sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis of Lévy exponents across overlapping time windows
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer positions, velocities, times, and diagnostics
		- Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step ΔW=2*T_L; compute ~26 windows
		- For each window: compute net displacement magnitudes |r(t+τ) - r(t)| at lags τ={0.5,1,2}*T_L; treat x and y components separately then pool to maximize sample size; exclude windows with fewer than 500 displacement samples per lag
		- Fit α via McCulloch quantile method: compute ν_α = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25); implement hardcoded McCulloch (1986) lookup table with bilinear interpolation in the (ν_α, ν_β) plane; clip α to [1.0, 2.0] after interpolation
		- Also compute Hill estimator α_Hill as cross-check using top-k order statistics
		- For each window: fit MSD(τ) power law to get γ(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get ν(t)
		- Extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics for each window center
		- Run ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on α(t), γ(t), ν(t) time series; print test statistics and p-values
		- Fit α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares; print fitted parameters with uncertainties
		- Save all window statistics (alpha_mcculloch, alpha_hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers, adf_results, kpss_results) to npz file
		- Print all fitted parameters, stationarity test p-values, and key statistics to console

- Step 4:
	* sub-task: Plot non-stationarity analysis results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load saved window statistics from Step 3
		- Generate multi-panel figure: α(t) with both McCulloch and Hill estimates shown, γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t), d_vv(t) all vs time window center
		- Generate scatter plot of α vs k_peak/k_box with fitted curve α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid; include confidence band
		- Annotate plots with ADF/KPSS test p-values and stationarity conclusions
		- Save plots to data directory

- Step 5:
	* sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy as memory-mapped array (not fully into RAM); load vel_times.npy
		- Apply 2D DWT using db4 wavelet (periodic boundary mode) to each velocity snapshot one at a time to avoid OOM; extract detail coefficients at levels j=1,...,6 using PyWavelets
		- Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q from 256×256 velocity snapshots using finite differences; compute masking threshold once from time-averaged |Q| field (75th percentile); apply mask Q < -Q_threshold when computing spatial averages for T_eddy
		- For each level j: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|²; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(τ,j) averaged over masked pixels; integrate until C_E < 0.1 (max lag = min(T_prod/4, 50*dt_vel)) to get T_eddy(j)
		- Reconstruct filtered velocity fields ũ_j for j_min ∈ {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; store as memory-mapped arrays or process in chunks
		- Re-advect 5000 tracers seeded at positions from tracer_initial_positions.npy through each filtered field using GPU-accelerated RK4; use vel_times.npy for temporal interpolation weights (not assuming uniform spacing); enforce periodic boundary wrapping via modulo 2π before each grid_sample call
		- For each filtered field: compute α(j) via McCulloch method, γ_MSD(j) from MSD power law, ν_VACF(j) from VACF decay
		- Save T_eddy(j), α(j), γ_MSD(j), ν_VACF(j) for all j to npz file
		- Print all T_eddy values, α(j), γ(j), ν(j) to console

- Step 6:
	* sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load wavelet results from Step 5 and window statistics from Step 3
		- Extract β(j) from α(j) via inverse relation β = 2*(α(j)-1)/α(j) (derived from α = 1+β/(2-β)); fit log(β) vs log(T_eddy) via linear regression to obtain scaling exponent δ; print δ with uncertainty
		- Plot α(j) vs T_eddy(j): show data points, direct power-law fit to α, and CTRW-predicted curve from β power law overlaid for visual comparison; annotate with δ
		- Plot γ_MSD(j) and ν_VACF(j) vs T_eddy(j) on separate panels
		- Synthesis panel: compare α(t) from Step 3 with α(j_peak(t)) from wavelet analysis at dominant scale j corresponding to k_peak(t) at each window center
		- Generate 2D heatmap of α as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box) using all available data points from both analyses
		- Save all synthesis plots to data directory

- Step 7:
	* sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
	* agent in charge: researcher

	* instructions:
		- Summarize DNS simulation parameters (N=1024, k_force∈[3,5], nu_h=1e-19, epsilon_inj=0.1, dt_snap=0.05) and spinup/production run outcomes (T_L value, k_peak evolution, U_rms)
		- Report data quality verification with numerical values: mean displacement per step vs threshold 1.88, VACF values at lags 1,2,4,8,16 steps, τ_corr value, and whether C_vv(τ=1) > 0.3 criterion was met
		- Describe inverse cascade development quantitatively: k_peak values at t=0.1,0.3,0.6,1.0×T_prod
		- Report non-stationarity analysis with full numerical results: α(t) range and mean, γ(t) range, ν(t) range; ADF test statistics and p-values for each series; KPSS test statistics and p-values; fitted parameters a, b, c with confidence intervals for α = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c; R² of fit
		- Report wavelet decomposition results: T_eddy(j) for j=1,...,6; α(j), γ(j), ν(j) for each filtered field; fitted scaling exponent δ with uncertainty; R² of β vs T_eddy fit; whether CTRW prediction α = 1+β/(2-β) is supported
		- Synthesis: quantitative comparison of α(t) vs α(j_peak(t)); interpretation of 2D α map; conclusion on stationarity of Lévy exponent
		- Quantitative comparison against prior v1 result α ≈ 1.4 ± 0.1: report whether v2 α values fall within or outside this band, and discuss physical interpretation of any differences

name: plan_recorder
content: Plan recorded as final. Planning stage complete.
